meanings.usage: 指公司或机构中处理文书和管理工作的人
描述人感到不安或紧张的状态
常与can/could连用，表示有能力支付或做某事
通常指按月或按年发放的固定工资
指在别人说话时打断对方
表示超出平常或标准的部分
表示数字1000，用于具体数额
指商业活动或公司经营的状况
my turn表示轮到某人的时候
指被动地获得或收到某物
形容声音小或身体不强壮
作动词时表示微笑的动作，作名词时表示微笑的表情
表示某事在说话之前已经发生或完成
指将视线从低处移向高处或从正在做的事抬起头

phrases.usage: 从...抬起头/停下手中的事抬头看

examples.en: He did not look up from his desk when I entered.
examples.zh: 我进去时他没有从桌子上抬起头。

examples.en: After I had sat down, he said that business was very bad.
examples.zh: 我坐下后，他说生意很糟糕。

examples.en: The firm could not afford to pay such large salaries.
examples.zh: 公司负担不起这么高的工资。

phrases.usage: 轮到我了/我的机会来了

examples.en: I knew that my turn had come.

phrases.usage: 用微弱的/怯生生的声音

examples.en: I said in a weak voice.

phrases.usage: 收到额外的.../获得额外的...

examples.en: I would receive an extra thousand pounds a year.
examples.zh: 我每年将额外获得一千英镑。

examples.en: The secretary told me that Mr. Harmsworth would see me.
examples.zh: 秘书告诉我哈姆斯沃斯先生要见我。
已经有二十个人离开了。

examples.en: I felt very nervous when I went into his office.
examples.zh: 我走进他办公室时感到很紧张。

grammar.title: 间接引语（Reported Speech）
grammar.definition: 间接引语是用自己的话转述别人的话，通常使用that引导的宾语从句，且时态要后退（过去时推进一位）。
grammar.structure: 主句动词（said/told）+ that + 从句
grammar.usage: 用于转述他人说过的话，常见的动词有say、tell、ask等。

examples.en: The secretary told me that Mr. Harmsworth would see me.
examples.zh: 秘书告诉我哈姆斯沃斯先生要见我。

examples.en: He said that business was very bad.
He told me I would receive an extra thousand pounds a year.
examples.zh: 他告诉我我每年将额外获得一千英镑。

grammar.title: 过去完成时（Past Perfect Tense）
grammar.definition: 过去完成时表示在过去某一时刻之前已经发生的动作或状态，由had + 过去分词构成。
grammar.usage: 用于描述两个过去的动作，先发生的动作用过去完成时，后发生的动作用一般过去时。

examples.en: After I had sat down, he said...

examples.zh: 已经有二十个人离开了。

grammar.title: would + 动词原形（将来过去时）
grammar.definition: 在间接引语中，原来的将来时态will变成would，表示从过去的角度看将要发生的事。
grammar.usage: 用于过去将来的情况，在转述时态后退的规则下，will变为would。

examples.zh: 哈姆斯沃斯先生将要见我。

examples.en: I would receive an extra thousand pounds a year.
examples.zh: 我将每年获得额外的一千英镑。

sentencePatterns.pattern: 主语 + told me that + 从句

original.en: The secretary told me that Mr. Harmsworth would see me.
original.zh: 秘书告诉我哈姆斯沃斯先生要见我。

imitations.en: My mother told me that I would get a promotion.
imitations.zh: 我妈妈告诉我我将获得晋升。

imitations.en: The teacher told us that the exam would be difficult.
imitations.zh: 老师告诉我们考试将会很难。

sentencePatterns.pattern: 主语 + couldn't afford to + 动词原形

original.en: The firm could not afford to pay such large salaries.
original.zh: 公司负担不起这么高的工资。

imitations.en: I couldn't afford to buy a new car.
She couldn't afford to travel abroad.

sentencePatterns.pattern: After + 主语 + had + 过去分词, 主语 + 过去式

original.en: After I had sat down, he said that business was very bad.
original.zh: 我坐下后，他说生意很糟糕。

imitations.en: After I had finished my homework, I went to bed.
imitations.zh: 我完成作业后就去睡觉了。

imitations.en: After he had left, we started the meeting.
imitations.zh: 他离开后，我们开始了会议。

sentencePatterns.pattern: 主语 + would receive + 金额 + a year/month

original.en: I would receive an extra thousand pounds a year!
original.zh: 我每年将额外获得一千英镑！

imitations.en: She would receive 500 dollars a month as allowance.
imitations.zh: 她每月将获得500美元作为零用钱。

imitations.en: He would receive a bonus of 2000 yuan a year.
imitations.zh: 他每年将获得2000元的奖金。
